name: Protection
image_description: A gentle mother dotes on her children. The waxing moon above with the symbol of the tiger below indicates the immense instinctive protective power of the feminine side of human nature.
personal: The combination of Moon and Aries indicates a powerful need to respond emotionally and immediately to a situation. This can make people with this planet/sign combination quite volatile and impulsive, expressing what they feel in a direct and willful manner. But because of the fluctuating nature of the Moon and the speed of action of Mars, quick flare-ups are fortunately just as quickly forgotten. The person this card describes has feelings that are strongly colored by early childhood conditioning, and an intense need to protect and defend anything that represents "family." Just as the Moon's cycle waxes and wanes, so the Moon/Aries nature can be somewhat moody and changeable, fluctuating with the environment. Often the Moon Arian has a strong impressionability that could indicate some psychic ability or potential for medium-like powers. There is a great deal of independence in the Moon/Aries combination, but if the negative side takes over, this card can describe an emotional bully. The Moon/Aries' need for recognition could also lead to approval-seeking.
reading: In a reading, this card might suggest that the questioner watch that sympathy with another's predicament does not leave him or her vulnerable to manipulation. One could find oneself having to protect one's position. The card may indicate a link with the questioner's talent for buying property, or running a business from home.
events: Defensive action, maternal instinct; business enterprise, arguments over property, reluctantly moving; family upsets or responsibility.

name: Exaltation
image_description: A waxing moon sends out floral rays above while below two garlanded cornucopias hold fruits of the earth.
personal: The Moon/Taurus combination demonstrates that to feel good emotionally, one has to feel secure, and security for this personality means material well-being. Luckily, Moon Taureans have a knack of making the things they need grow and grow, as long as they are comfortable with their feelings. Usually, the Moon/Taurus temperament is steady and placid, and a stable home and family life is of paramount importance to the well-being of Moon Taureans: without this, many succumb to over-eating as a compensation. This card could be said to be the "green fingers" card, since the Taurean love of beauty combined with the Moon's nurturing qualities make for many excellent gardeners with the Moon/Taurus combination. In astrology, the placing of the Moon in Taurus is regarded as excellent, and is traditionally known as the Moon's exaltation. This means that it is allowed full expression in this sign and usually bodes well for any question that is the subject of the reading.
reading: This card appears when strong hunches about making money are about to pay off. It may indicate that business possibilities to do with food, catering, property or a business operated from home will be successful. The card can warn of the typical Taurean danger of self-indulgence, which can spoil or upset any good situation.
events: Possession of something beautiful, an excessive attachment to material comforts; inheriting a piece of family property; artistic expression that proves profitable; financial worries that may or may not be justified; a good business opportunity.

name: Adaptability
image_description: Two strange performers. Above, a four-armed figure holds a sun-moon, a dove, a growing shrub and a flower. He stands on the back of another man balancing an ankh on his foot. Beneath, a boat is supported by four replicas of the magicians' Ape of Thoth.
personal: The Moon/Gemini personality rationalizes emotions, and his or her need to talk and explore the whys and wherefores of feelings can make for expensive phone bills. Early childhood memories will, to a large extent, condition the current thoughts and ideas of people with this planet/sign combination. Moon Geminians have an intensely strong imagination and sense of fantasy coupled with a very good memory. Restless curiosity keeps this personality type constantly on the move, avoiding monotony at all costs. Spontaneous quick-wittedness and an ability to come up with the right answer most of the time is tiring, though, and nerves always suffer. The negative aspect of this placing of the Moon is a tendency towards nervous troubles and fidgety feet. Too many ideas and concerns running round inside the head at the same time is no way to solve problems: the person the Adaptability card describes may even forget what he or she felt in the first place. The need to talk everything out, although of prime importance, must also be balanced with periods of mental relaxation.
reading: When this card is drawn, close relatives, brothers and sisters will be prominent in the life of the questioner, and will always be a part of the response. Negatively, the Adaptability card indicates a period of fidgeting during which it will be almost impossible to relax.
events: Important discussions with relations; taking examinations; confused thinking; information changes; secretive comments; laying one's cards on the table.

name: Friendship
image_description: Above, children play in a wheatfield among the cornflowers. They share the delight in the flight of the heron. Below, either side of a crescent moon symbolizing fruitfulness, are two ancient alchemical symbols of the moon metal, silver.
personal: The Moon is the natural ruler of the sign Cancer, and people with this planet/sign combination are comfortable with their emotions: moreover, somewhat like a mother hen, they seem able to feel everybody else's as well. To Moon Cancerians, the world really is how they feel it to be; if that turns out to be tough, they can hard-shell those around them with their out-and-out defensive crabbiness. Emotionally, Moon Cancerians are at their best when all is well with home and family. For them, family is all: friends become family, and family become friends. The early influence of parents, whether enjoyed or not, remains a strong influence throughout life. This position of the Moon also shows the potential for becoming a good cook or at least a lover of good food. However, upset emotions can cause Cancerians digestive troubles that may well reverse this indication, focussing concentration instead on nurturing the family.
reading: When this card occurs in a reading, it indicates a shrewd business ability, if the questioner dares launch out on his or her own. Often when the Friendship card occurs, it highlights an almost psychic sensitivity to the thoughts and feelings of others: the person asking the question may be called upon in the future to act as a shoulder to cry on for a close friend or a family member.
events: Situations such as important family gatherings and celebrations of anniversaries; inheritance of heirlooms, genealogy, distant relations; moving house; sharing a friend or family member's problems.

name: Appreciation
image_description: Above, a rich and elegant lady performs on a lute. Her audience is a swift symbolizing spring and new creation. Below is a cornucopia bursting with fruit symbolizing man and woman, Sun and Moon.
personal: The emotions of somewhat prima-donna-ish Moon/Leo characters require affection, appreciation, and lots of good opportunities for over-dramatic expression. This card indicates a need to be center stage as regards feelings, because for Leos big is beautiful, and they can be as emotionally generous as they are bullying. Emotionally, Moon/Leo people are great romantics. Feelings are important to them and must be appreciated and respected if Moon lions are to maintain their usual warm optimism. Children can be an important part of their life and, whether they have any of their own or not, people with this planet/sign combination are fond of the company of young-thinking people. This basically emotionally optimistic person has a tendency to over-impulsive actions where loved ones are concerned. He or she often has a good natural feeling for art and can benefit from developing latent talents in this area.
reading: When this card appears in a reading, it can indicate a powerful need for recognition in some aspect of life. There may be a feeling that those around are not responding appreciatively enough, provoking an unhelpful over-the-top response. The card can also indicate emotional dependency on a partner or close friends. Positively, if the card does not refer to the questioner, it can portray someone popular, generous and much admired by others.
events: A lavish occasion; theatrical event, children's performance; pride in an achievement; short-lived fame, excessively dramatic behavior; family party or gathering.

name: Order
image_description: Above are three neat rows of growing plants and three symbols representing order in nature and design. Below, a strip of brocade suggests patient and delicate needlework.
personal: Moon Virgos like to keep emotions tidy and private. Eschewing big scenes and showy expressions of feelings, the person this card describes prefers sincerity, modesty and quiet forms of expression. Nevertheless, he or she shows emotions in practical and material ways. Sometimes the Moon/Virgo card reveals an aptitude for work in catering and food preparation. The modest side to the Virgoan's emotions makes it necessary that they keep all feelings on an even keel. Ups and downs do not suit the Virgoan need for order and consistency. Outbursts of feeling or sentiment by others always embarrass this character. There is, of course, a danger with this Moon placing that one may get hurt by other people, who have little regard for feelings so minimally shown. To keep on good form, the person this card describes should seek to find some form of balance. Emotional well-being is important to the health of a Virgoan, and upsets inevitably lead to pill-popping. Diet and health are important, too, with this Moon placing, and if the person described is not on top of emotions, he or she could become a fastidious, fussy health crank. With the Virgoan drive for perfection, this card suggests the danger of becoming over-critical and nagging.
reading: When this card appears in a reading, it may indicate a tidying up of work systems and health issues. It shows that good healthy diet regimes and new, efficient, day-to-day work procedures are necessary or desirable.
events: A new health regime; family responsibility, social work or organization; background work or research.

name: Companionship
image_description: Above, four classical feminine figures link arms in friendship. Below, hands grasp in greeting; the pomegranate is a symbol of the Sun and indicates fertility and growth.
personal: The emotions of Moon/Libra people are invariably influenced by those around them. They really care how others are feeling, and can be quickly upset when all is not harmonious. Librans are vulnerable to immersing their own emotional needs in someone else's difficulties. The biggest problem this card indicates is when a powerful desire to please and keep everything in balance emotionally leads to someone giving in under tricky situations in order to maintain harmony. Environment is an important influence on Moon Librans, who feel the need for an atmosphere of good taste and beauty. The Libran emotional ideal is to feel at one with a partner in a harmonious setting. Life is rarely as perfect as that, and this character may feel the constant need to act as peacemaker between others. It is good to swing the balance occasionally, and risk a bit of selfishness in order not to get sat on so frequently. How other people feel and act affects people with this planet/sign combination more than most, and they might be watchful not to fall into the trap of allowing themselves to be treated like a doormat.
reading: When this card is chosen, it often indicates a marriage or relationship entered into purely for emotional and domestic security. Because of the Moon's link with the home and Libra's usual good taste, the Companionship card will indicate someone who has a beautiful home.
events: Beautifying the home; discussions about business or contracts, seminars; teamwork, new friendships; social meetings.

name: Power
image_description: Above, a stone gateway indicates feminine power, and behind, black hills represent challenge and ambition. The sun and moon unite. Below; a woman lies dreaming.
personal: Moon/Scorpio people have very strong feelings and form lasting emotional attachments. Intensely loyal, they are, however, susceptible to brooding jealousy and strong emotional outbursts. The Moon is traditionally in its "fall" in Scorpio, and so this is not a sign in which emotions are freely expressed. Often, an immense conservation of emotional energy is released in an intense and extreme manner. People with this combination can hold a grudge longer than most, but find no sacrifice too great for a loved one. Moon Scorpios can at times be overwhelmed by intense emotional reactions and an almost psychic sensitivity to others. These qualities are helpful in developing an aptitude for healing and counseling. Certainly with all this emotional energy, Moon Scorpions find some kind of work helping the less fortunate fulfilling. Family matters are rarely smooth for people with this aspect, often involving seemingly causeless separations. Moon Scorpios are potentially very sensual, and when over-indulged, this can lead to problems. With the extreme emotional power that this combination engenders, the old adage that Scorpios make the most powerful friends and most destructive enemies becomes a reality.
reading: When this card is drawn, it usually involves emotional fireworks – passion or hate, enthusiasm or indifference, there is rarely a middle ground. Often, the card indicates an inheritance that could radically change life.
events: Powerful emotional situations; a secret suddenly revealed; psychological problems; an inheritance; love turning to hate or vice versa.

name: Optimism
image_description: Above, a damsel rides a pair of sea horses. The horse is Sagittarius's beast and the fish tails represent the watery Moon. In the sky is a cherub leading the voodoo ritual diagram (vèvè) that conjures truth, justice and love ruled by reason.
personal: Moon Sagittarians are somewhat idealistic emotionally: they express feelings honestly, and expect others to be equally straightforward. Usually confident, people with this planet/sign combination can, unthinkingly, be crushingly gauche with their frankness. It is important for Sagittarians to say what they understand to be true at the risk of hurting the feelings of other people, believing that their honesty is more important to relationships than well-meaning white lies. Moon/Sagittarius types are far-thinking and always have their sights set on distant lands and truths. Journeying is their pleasure, and their optimism is unbounded. Emotions are high when they are on the move, traveling physically or mentally. They probably still have a deep, unconscious emotional attachment to the religious, social and ethical values inherited from family during childhood. Though these beliefs may now manifest as something more unorthodox, the spiritual side of life is of great significance to Moon Sagittarians. Negatively, the person this card describes may reflect a narrow and dogmatic social or religious outlook; the Optimism card may describe an extreme and immovable fundamentalist.
reading: When this card is chosen, it always indicates that a long trip abroad is imminent or that a visit from someone from the past or a friend from a distant country will be important to the person asking the question.
events: A move to another country, exploring foreign philosophy, planning a holiday; a family project; contact from the past; a reason for optimism.

name: Practicality
image_description: Above, a rustic scene represents order and practicality, with ruler and servants; tillers of the soil and millers of wheat. Below; a pattern alludes to worker bees and the Moon lotus flowers that bring forth honey.
personal: The Moon/Capricorn character can be a little cautious and restrained in expressing true emotions. They always seek practical and material answers to emotional problems; rarely will this person show what he or she feels to be weak expressions of feelings in any situation. People with this planet/sign combination care how other people see them, and tend to hold in feelings in order to maintain an image of outward calm. The position of the Moon in this sign also indicates an over-sensitive reaction to imagined or real slights from others. The Moon Capricornian's position in society contributes to his or her emotional well-being, and people with this planet/sign aspect usually have an emotional need for prominence and recognition, and may have a family history of achievement to live up to. Whatever degree of achievement this is, Moon Capricornians have strong family links, with the mother in particular representing a profound influence.
reading: Drawing this card suggests that the person to whom it refers will be brought prominently before the public eye. It indicates ability in the political or community field; if this is not already part of life, it could become important. The card suggests exceptional organization abilities that need a vehicle to bring about success. Often the Practicality card indicates the assistance of a prominent or successful woman in advancing the career of the person asking the question.
events: Social work; practical assistance; recognition for patient and unassuming work, achievement through diligence.

name: Independence
image_description: Above, on a plinth in front of the circular plan of the British temple Stonehenge, an Egyptian female royal figure. To either side are the sign of the Water-carrier and a waxing crescent Moon. The Menhirs of Carnac, France, indicate the yearly cycles of Sun and Moon.
personal: Moon Aquarians require complete freedom of expression for their feelings, coming and going as they please physically and emotionally. Often, Aquarians are stubborn or just perverse when others get a little too close and clingy. They switch emotions quickly, from friend to friend and mood to mood. Friendship on a broad level keeps Moon Aquarians emotionally high. This means plenty of friends, plenty of new contacts, and a great deal of conversation. Lasting friendships with women are more than usually important for people with this aspect, reflecting the feminine Moon placed in the sign. The Aquarian desire for constant contact with others is valued in order to exchange, form and reform ideas, the bond being more usually with the mind than with the heart. This card describes someone whose feelings and emotions tend to be rationalized and thought over interminably. Moon/Aquarius is also traditionally a planet/sign combination that indicates an astrologer.
reading: Often when this card occurs in a reading, it shows that the person asking the question is trapped in a mental whirlwind. It indicates a need to let go of emotions and to allow feelings to express themselves without rationalizing them. It also suggests that the questioner's home will be used in the future for group meetings with some kind of good cause or humanitarian subject.
events: Some kind of unconventional assistance, original ideas brought to bear on a situation or a subject; a second opinion canvassed; an interest in astrology.

name: Empathy
image_description: Above, in a charming country scene with a winding river, a child pets a large dog, the two enjoying just being together. Below; a golden-bird ornament symbolizes the spirit image linked to the material – gold. The ear represents an openness to calls for help.
personal: People with a Moon/Pisces personality display a supersensitive emotional nature that acts like a psychic sponge, soaking up the feelings and thoughts of others. In fact, the way in which people with this planet/sign combination feel responds very strongly to the vibes they pick up from others. Easily hurt, but emotionally resilient, the Moon Piscean often has to be helped to be able to express feelings fully. Emotions for Pisceans are related strongly to the unconscious and to past experience. Whether they have experimented with psychic abilities or not, they undoubtedly have great potential for development in this area. The Moon in Pisces card describes someone with a deep underlying shyness that contrasts with a great sensitivity and an ability to make contact with other people. This card can often also indicate an intuitive ability for fashion in all areas, and highlights a good business aspect when hunches are played with a degree of realism.
reading: When this card appears in a reading, it suggests that the questioner has more concern for the feelings of others than for his or her own emotions. The Empathy card also indicates a very good feeling for public taste that can be exploited by the development of a new service or product that satisfies a general need.
events: Situations such as a hidden mystery, a powerful dream or a premonition; a tendency to escape from situations through alcohol and drugs; the need to make a sacrifice; an ability to tap into the public mood or taste to one's advantage.
